faqs.a: In cashless, the insurer settles the hospital bill directly with a network hospital, so you don't pay upfront (except for items not covered by the policy). You must be admitted to a network hospital and have pre-authorization approved.

faqs.q: how to find a network hospital
faqs.a: Network hospitals are listed in the Sampoorna app under 'Find a Hospital'. Cashless works only at these hospitals; at other hospitals you pay and claim reimbursement later.
Pre-authorization is the hospital sending your diagnosis, treatment plan, and cost estimate to the insurer for approval before treatment begins. It is required for cashless.
The hospital submits your policy number, the doctor's treatment plan, the diagnosis, and an estimated cost. The hospital insurance desk usually handles most of this for you.
For planned admissions, approval is usually given within a few hours of the hospital submitting the request. For emergencies it can be faster.
If cashless pre-auth is denied, you can still pay the hospital and file for reimbursement later with the required documents. A pre-auth denial is not the same as a claim denial.
For planned admission, request pre-authorization 48-72 hours before. For emergency admission, the hospital can request pre-authorization within 24 hours of admission.
I can check the status of your cashless pre-authorization if you give me your policy number.

faqs.q: can I use cashless at any hospital
faqs.a: No. Cashless is only available at network hospitals. For non-network hospitals you must pay yourself and then claim reimbursement.

faqs.q: do I pay anything in cashless
faqs.a: You may still have to pay for items the policy doesn't cover - such as non-medical consumables, or any amount above a sub-limit or room rent cap.
A co-pay is a share of the bill you pay yourself. This plan has no co-pay, so the insurer covers the eligible amount fully, subject to limits.
Go to a network hospital, show your health card or policy details at the insurance desk, and ask them to raise a pre-authorization request. I can guide you through it.

faqs.q: what if my treatment cost is more than approved
faqs.a: If the final bill is higher than the approved estimate, the hospital sends an enhancement request to the insurer during your stay for the extra amount.
Yes, ambulance charges are covered up to Rs 2,000 per claim under this plan.
Yes, day-care procedures that don't need 24-hour hospitalization are covered under this plan.
